mood: austere, pressurized, the still center of a moving storm
name: Washington's Forward Command Post, Brooklyn Ferry Road
description: A shuttered lantern on a wooden crate beside the road to the ferry slip, where Washington receives runners and scratches tally marks on damp paper. The command post is a man standing in the mud with a quill, not a tent.
atmosphericDetails: The scratch of a quill on damp paper as Washington updates his running count of regiments crossed.
The sound of boots on the muddy road as runners arrive from the parapet and the slip in alternating sequence.
The cold weight of a rain-soaked wool coat and the smell of wet horse from the tethered mounts nearby.
The faint, recurring sound of the gale gusting off the river, rattling the shuttered lantern on its hook.
